summary: User policy for third-party suggestions in Windows Spotlight. Good for a less promotional experience, but it removes recommendation surfaces that can point to third-party content or apps.

setting.name: Third-Party Suggestions in Windows Spotlight
setting.casual_explanation: Windows Spotlight can include suggestions that point to third-party content or apps. This policy decides whether those suggestions are allowed for the current user.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it to reduce third-party promotion and keep Windows from recommending outside content through Spotlight.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off reduces promotional suggestions, but it can also remove discovery of some third-party content or app recommendations.
setting.professional_notes: The Experience Policy CSP uses the allow-form name AllowThirdPartySuggestionsInWindowsSpotlight, while CloudContent.admx maps the user Group Policy to DisableThirdPartySuggestions with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the third-party Spotlight suggestions policy for the current user.

allowed_values.label: Third-party Spotlight suggestions allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and Windows Spotlight can show third-party suggestions.

allowed_values.label: Third-party Spotlight suggestions disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Windows Spotlight won't show third-party suggestions for the current user.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableThirdPartySuggestions = 1, which matches the documented Group Policy disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default

states.rationale: The user policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The Experience Policy CSP documents the allow-form default as allowed, while the registry policy baseline remains not configured unless the policy is set.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who are comfortable with Spotlight recommendations
avoid_for: Users who want a less promotional experience
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows Spotlight to keep its ordinary recommendation behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to stop third-party suggestions from appearing through Spotlight.
tradeoffs: No policy-based restriction is enforced for this content path.

recommended_profiles.label: Quiet UI
avoid_for: Users who want Spotlight to surface broader recommendations

states.rationale: Turn off third-party Spotlight suggestions through Group Policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows Spotlight to stop surfacing third-party suggestions.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Windows to keep those recommendation surfaces available.
tradeoffs: Some discovery of outside content or apps may disappear.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: AllowThirdPartySuggestionsInWindowsSpotlight
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the allow-form policy controlling third-party suggestions in Windows Spotlight.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft CloudContent.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the user Group Policy to DisableThirdPartySuggestions with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the CloudContent policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableThirdPartySuggestions = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Local Microsoft CloudContent policy files document the exact user-policy surface, numeric mapping, and third-party Spotlight suggestion behavior. The app writes the documented disabled value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="DisableThirdPartySuggestions" class="User" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent" valueName="DisableThirdPartySuggestions">; CloudContent.adml: If you enable this policy, Windows spotlight features like lock screen spotlight, suggested apps in Start menu or Windows tips will no longer suggest apps and content from third-party software publishers.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact user-policy key and value name; local ADML confirms that enabling the policy stops Windows Spotlight from suggesting third-party apps and content.
